title: Seung-Hye Kim
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: Seung-Hye Kim
Snapshot: 2026-07-21
biography: Seung-Hye Kim is a pianist, organist and composer. She received a PhD in Music Composition from the University of Florida, an MA in Electroacoustic Music Composition from the Korean National University of the Arts, and a BA in Piano Performance from Seoul National University. Her compositions have been performed in various festivals and conferences in the US and abroad. Her composition “Residuum” for marimba duo won an award in a competition staged by the Italy Percussive Art Society, and her piece “Fluctuations” for flute and computer has been released on the SEAMUS label. She has collaborated with video artists and dancers to create realtime audio visual installations/performances. One of her collaborations “Global Drift” was performed in Brisbane, Australia as part of the international large-scale artistic project “Accented Body”. As an active performer, her repertoire includes classical and contemporary works as well as works for piano and computer. Previously, she has taught at Korean National University of the Arts and University of Florida.
education_entries: Ph D in Music Composition, University of Florida
MA in Electroacoustic Music Composition, Kolrean National University of Arts
BA in Piano Performance, Seoul National University
research_interests: Contemporary Music, Contemporary Piano Music, Acoustic and Electroacoustic Music
